channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump and Xi commence high-level summit in Beijing with formal welcome ceremony
item.description: US President Donald Trump has arrived in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. The visit began with a formal welcome ceremony at the Great Hall of the People, which inclu...

item.title: PNP Confirms Equal Treatment for Bato dela Rosa Amid ICC Warrant
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) clarified on Thursday that Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa will not receive any special treatment following the issuance of an International Criminal Court (ICC) a...

item.title: Super Eagles Coach Chelle Recognised for Scouting Talents in Nigeria League
item.description: Chief Christopher Okonkwo, a respected football pundit for the State, don give big kudos to Super Eagles Coach Eric Chelle as e dey waka from one Naija Professional Football League ground go another d...

item.title: West Bengal government clears CBI to investigate corruption in recruitment scams
item.description: The West Bengal government on Wednesday granted the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) permission to prosecute state government officials accused of corruption, particularly in recruitment scams sp...

item.title: Varsho hits a decisive grand slam to secure Blue Jays win against Rays
item.description: In a dramatic 10th-inning showdown on Wednesday, Daulton Varsho delivered a clutch grand slam to propel the Toronto Blue Jays past the Tampa Bay Rays with a 5-3 victory. The win came at a critical mom...

item.title: Law Minister Clarifies No Move to Repeal 18th Amendment Amid Speculation
item.description: ISLAMABAD: State Minister for Law Barrister Aqeel Malik has clarified that there is no formal plan to reverse the 18th Constitutional Amendment, dismissing reports suggesting that a 28th Amendment cou...

item.title: Communities Urged to Support Those Affected by Severe Storms in Western and Eastern Cape
item.description: South Africans have witnessed severe storms wreak havoc across parts of the Western Cape and Eastern Cape, with destructive winds exceeding 110km/h causing widespread damage. Trucks, containers, and r...

item.title: A Glimpse Into Mid-19th Century Men's Fashion Through Vintage Photos
item.description: Menswear in the mid-19th century experienced a pivotal transformation, moving away from the flamboyant styles of the Romantic era toward a more structured and refined aesthetic. This period saw the fr...

item.title: CAF Introduces New Regulations to Prevent AFCON Final Chaos
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) don bring new rules after the gbege wey happen for the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) final wey hold for Morocco. CAF president Patrice Motsepe talk say...

item.title: Fish and chip shops adopt self-service tills due to rising costs and customer pressure
item.description: Fish and chip shops across Pembrokeshire are responding to rising ingredient costs and customer pressure by introducing self-service tills. Rhys McLoughlin, co-owner of Môr Ffres in Dinas Cross, state...

item.title: Mapua Shows Promising Performance in Philippine Youth Basketball Championship U18
item.description: Mapua University made an impressive start in the Philippine Youth Basketball Championship (PYBC) Under-18 division, showcasing their skills and teamwork early in the tournament. The team displayed a s...

item.title: Authentic Photobooth Portraits Capturing a Young Woman in the 1920s
item.description: Long before smartphones and selfies, photobooths offered a private and intimate space for self-expression. In the 1920s, a young woman named Evelyn used these tiny booths to reveal her true personalit...

item.title: ASUS Unveils Compact ProArt RTX 5090 OC GPU with Dual-Fan Cooling and Liquid Metal
item.description: ASUS has introduced the ProArt RTX 5090 OC Edition, a high-end graphics card designed for creators and small form factor (SFF) systems. The GPU features a sleek black and gold design with a 2.5-slot d...

item.title: The Fashion Photography Legacy of Chaloner Woods
item.description: Chaloner Woods (1903–1986) was a celebrated British fashion photographer whose work helped define the elegance and sophistication of 1950s and 1960s fashion. Renowned for his sharp eye and precise com...

item.title: China Imports 1.4 Million Barrels of Iranian Oil Daily
item.description: Analysts estimate that China imports approximately 1.4 million barrels of oil per day from Iran, representing about 12% of its total oil imports. This substantial volume underscores China's role as th...

item.title: New Linux Local Privilege Escalation Vulnerability 'Fragnesia' Disclosed with Patch Pending
item.description: A new Linux vulnerability named Fragnesia has been publicly disclosed, following in the footsteps of previous flaws like Dirty Frag. The bug allows local attackers to perform arbitrary byte writes int...

item.title: Early Career Highlights and Portraits of Shannen Doherty in the 1980s
item.description: Shannen Doherty (April 12, 1971 – July 13, 2024) began her acting career as a child in the early 1980s, quickly becoming a recognizable talent in television and film. At age 10, she made her debut on ...

item.title: Prince Harry voices concern over rise in antisemitic incidents across the UK
item.description: The Duke of Sussex, Prince Harry, has expressed significant concern over a noticeable increase in antisemitic incidents in the United Kingdom. He highlighted that such a rise is deeply troubling for s...

item.title: Flooding at Heathrow Airport leaves several parked cars partially submerged
item.description: A section of a car park and an adjacent road at London Heathrow Airport was recently flooded due to a localised water issue, affecting numerous vehicles. Footage circulating on social media showed sev...

item.title: James Carville Comments on Trump's Cognitive Decline and Behavior
item.description: During a recent episode of the Politicon podcast, Democratic strategist James Carville discussed former President Donald Trump's apparent cognitive decline. Carville responded to comments by host Al H...
